title: diplodium alatum
intro: Diplodium alatum, also known as the alpine or terrestrial orchid, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Diplodium alatum include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and potentially managing blood sugar levels. Herbal preparations of Diplodium alatum can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
